title: Chocolate orange marble cake
subtitle: Swirled chocolate and orange loaf cake with a fine, moist crumb
excerpt: A marble cake: a single Victoria sponge batter divided into two halves, one with orange zest and one with cocoa, then swirled in a loaf tin to create the marble effect. Simple to make, striking to cut. The orange and chocolate balance each other without either dominating.
sourceNotes: Marble cake (Marmorkuchen) is documented in German domestic baking from the nineteenth century, with recipes appearing in English-language guides shortly after. The chocolate-and-vanilla swirled loaf is the most widely reproduced form. The chocolate-orange variation replaces the vanilla half with orange zest, a combination documented in British domestic baking guides of the twentieth century. The technique of swirling two batters in a tin is fully public-domain.

recipe.freezeNotes: Slice before freezing. Defrost at room temperature.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps wrapped for 4 days. Flavour improves on day 2.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, 50 to 55 minutes until a skewer comes out clean

glossaryTerms.definition: The marble effect requires two batters that are distinct enough to look different but similar enough in consistency to swirl without one sinking through the other. Drop alternate spoonfuls of each batter into the loaf tin rather than layering in sections. To swirl, drag a skewer or thin knife through the batters in a figure-of-eight pattern two or three times. Fewer swirls give a bolder marble with large patches of colour; too many swirls blend the colours into a dull khaki. The key is to drag, not stir — a slow, deliberate figure-of-eight rather than rapid mixing.

content.text: Marble cake is a single batter divided into two bowls. There is no extra complexity — just the discipline to swirl gently rather than mix. The result should show bold, distinct patches of orange sponge and chocolate sponge when cut.

items.prepNote: zest only, for the orange half
2 tbsp, for loosening the chocolate half

content.text: Preheat the oven to 170°C fan. Grease and line a 900 g loaf tin. Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} and {{caster-sugar}} until pale and fluffy. Add the {{eggs}} one at a time. Fold in the {{self-raising-flour}}. Divide the batter equally between two bowls. Stir the zest of the {{orange}} into one bowl. Mix the {{cocoa-powder}} and {{whole-milk}} into a smooth paste and fold into the second bowl.
Drop alternate spoonfuls of orange and chocolate batter into the prepared tin. See the

method: drag a skewer through the batter in a figure-of-eight two or three times. Bake for 50 to 55 minutes until a skewer inserted in the centre comes out clean. Cool in the tin for 15 minutes before turning out.
